summary: El túnel de Rubí (Barcelona) estará siete semanas cortado y las conexiones internacionales no tienen otro paso alternativo. Se trata de una infraestructura que tiene unos 40 años y que, pese a ello, sobresale por su deterioro. Leer

title: Netanyahu pide acabar con el "circo absurdo" de su juicio por corrupción y centrarse en Irán

title: El nuevo líder supremo de Irán pide a los países vecinos que cierren las bases de EEUU en Oriente Medio
summary: Mojtaba Jameneí desafía a Estados Unidos y pide cerrar Ormuz en su primer mensaje a través de un comunicado leído por una presentadora en la televisión estatal. Leer

title: Sleepless Morrissey blames Spain's noisy Fallas for cancelling gig
summary: British singer Morrissey on Thursday cancelled his concert in Valencia due to "lack of sleep" caused by the noise of the famous Fallas festival, currently underway in the eastern Spanish city.

title: The new rules for discounted or free prosthetics and wheelchairs in Spain
summary: A royal decree widening access to healthcare in Spain will also align co-payment systems, expanding access to discounted and free prosthetics and wheelchairs for around 7 million people in the country.

title: Spain cites Israeli 'insults' in decision to withdraw ambassador
summary: Spain on Thursday explained its decision to permanently withdraw its ambassador to Israel, citing repeated "insults and slanders" by the country.

title: Spain had wettest January and February in nearly 50 years
summary: Spain endured its wettest January and February in almost half a century, with a string of deadly storms lashing the country, national weather agency Aemet said Thursday.

title: Far-right rise looms over regional vote in Spain's Castilla y León
summary: Spain's scandal-hit governing Socialists and the conservative opposition will seek to fend off an ascendant far right in a regional vote in Castile and Leon on Sunday that could be an indicator for national elections due next year.

title: When will Spain's €2,400 yearly benefit per child become available?
summary: Spain's planned universal child benefit has been lauded as necessary to eradicate child poverty and encourage Spaniards to form a family. So how likely is it to be approved this year?

title: Spain shocked after fire kills three women in gender violence case
summary: Spain was on Wednesday shocked by one of its worst episodes of gender-based violence as police investigated a suspected arson attack on a residential building that left three women dead and four other people injured.

title: Today in Spain: A roundup of the latest news on Thursday
summary: Zelensky to visit Spain 'in coming days', chemistry experiment injures three children in Catalonia, EU to punish Spain for snubbing VAT rules for self-employed and more news on Thursday March 12th.

title: 'Who profits from it?' Spain launches social media hate speech monitor
summary: Spanish Socialist Prime Minister Pedro Sánchez announced Wednesday the creation of a tool designed to measure the presence of hate speech on digital platforms and to hold accountable the tech billionaires who allow it to flourish.
